arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i llvm | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading the debug symbol file from Microsoft symbol server
# Build ID: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
cd /tmp/sv-c2048764c315423fa831ede63e138de8

# Try several URL patterns used by Microsoft symbol server for ELF debug info
for pattern in \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"; do
  echo "Trying: $pattern"
  code=$(curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -L "$pattern")
  echo "  HTTP $code"
  if [ "$code" = "200" ]; then
    echo "  SUCCESS - downloading..."
    curl -sS -L -o symbols_download "$pattern"
    file symbols_download
    break
  fi
done
arguments.description: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
Now I need to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames from `libmonosgen-2.0.so`. The Build ID is `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`.

I need to:
1. Find the debug symbols for this library using the Build ID
2. Use those symbols to resolve the PC addresses to function names

The frames I need to symbolicate are:

**Thread 7020 (main/crash thread):**
- #01 pc 0x222098 in libmonosgen-2.0.so


- #02 pc 0x2241ac in libmonosgen-2.0.so
- #03 pc 0x1abc80 in libmonosgen-2.0.so

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i llvm | head -5

# Try downloading the debug symbol file from Microsoft symbol server
# Build ID: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
cd /tmp/sv-c2048764c315423fa831ede63e138de8

# Try several URL patterns used by Microsoft symbol server for ELF debug info
for pattern in \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"; do
  echo "Trying: $pattern"
  code=$(curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -L "$pattern")
  echo "  HTTP $code"
  if [ "$code" = "200" ]; then
    echo "  SUCCESS - downloading..."
    curl -sS -L -o symbols_download "$pattern"
    file symbols_download
    break
  fi
done

data.content:
Here are the symbolicated `.NET runtime` frames across all threads:

**Thread 7020 (main — crash thread, SIGABRT):**
| Frame | PC | Symbol | Source |
|-------|-----|--------|--------|
| #01 | `0x222098` | `ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast` | `icall.c:6244` |
| #02 | `0x2241ac` | `ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast_raw` | `icall-def.h:202` |
| #03 | `0x1abc80` | `do_icall` | `interp/interp.c:2457` |
| #04 | `0x16de04` | `handle_isinst` | `type-checking.c:528` |

**Thread 7046 (SGen worker):**
| Frame | PC | Symbol | Source |
|-------|-----|--------|--------|
| #02 | `0x2f4b20` | `interp.c:?` (compiler-generated label `$d.147`) | — |
| #03 | `0x2f4c84` | `interp.c:?` (compiler-generated label `$d.147`) | — |

**Thread 7048 (Finalizer):**
| Frame | PC | Symbol | Source |
|-------|-----|--------|--------|
| #02 | `0x2c1a40` | `pin_major_object` | `sgen-marksweep.c:1166` |

**Summary:** The crash originated from `Environment.FailFast` being called through the Mono interpreter (`do_icall` → `ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast`), triggered during a type-check operation (`handle_isinst`). The other threads show normal GC activity — the SGen worker and Finalizer were in routine garbage collection operations when the abort occurred.
